title: Progress bar component for React UIs
description: Determinate Progress component for React and Next.js—show upload completion, wizard steps, and background jobs with accessible values.

og.title: Progress · Zentauri UI
og.description: Determinate Progress component for React and Next.js—show upload completion, wizard steps, and background jobs with accessible values.

twitter.title: Progress · Zentauri UI
twitter.description: Determinate Progress component for React and Next.js—show upload completion, wizard steps, and background jobs with accessible values.

intro: Progress bars communicate how far a task has advanced—uploads, installs, or wizard steps. Integrate them into React dashboards and Next.js status pages with clear textual values.
useCases: File uploads with byte counts and ETA copy.
Background sync jobs in admin consoles.
Onboarding checklists tied to measurable tasks.

faqs.question: Does the Progress component work with Next.js App Router?
faqs.answer: Yes. Import it like any other React component; keep interactive subtrees in client components when you need hooks or browser APIs, and leave static structure in server components where possible.

faqs.question: Can I customize progress with Tailwind CSS?
faqs.answer: Zentauri UI exposes class-friendly variants and slots so you can extend styles with Tailwind utilities without fighting inline styles.

faqs.question: Is this Progress implementation accessible by default?
faqs.answer: Primitives follow sensible defaults, but accessibility depends on how you label controls, manage focus, and wire keyboard handlers in your app. Validate critical flows with keyboard-only use and screen readers.

sections.heading: What it does
sections.body:
Pair visuals with aria-valuenow updates for assistive technologies.

Avoid fake progress that stalls—users lose trust quickly.

sections.heading: Composition and API
sections.body:
Prefer compound subcomponents instead of one oversized prop bag. Export a small, documented API for your design system.

Reset to indeterminate spinners when duration is unknown.

sections.heading: Accessibility
sections.body:
Keyboard order, focus rings, and ARIA attributes should match production usage. Test with your supported browsers and assistive technologies when semantics are non-trivial.

This preview page exposes a single h1 in the hero for a clean outline.

sections.heading: Next.js integration notes
sections.body:
Colocate examples under the App Router, keep server and client boundaries explicit, and avoid pulling interactive overlays into unexpected server layouts.

Set NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL so canonical and Open Graph URLs resolve on deploy.
